name: The 2000s
description: The 2000s — pop culture, tech, and world events

questions.q: Who sang the 2003 pop hit 'Toxic'?

source.notes: From her fourth studio album 'In the Zone'.

questions.q: Nelly had a huge 2002 dance-floor hit called what?

source.notes: Reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2002.

questions.q: Which Australian pop star had a 2001 global smash with 'Can't Get You Out of My Head'?

source.notes: Features the iconic 'La la la' hook.

questions.q: In what year were the September 11 attacks in the United States?

source.notes: Four coordinated attacks by al-Qaeda.

questions.q: Which boy band, featuring JC Chasez and Justin Timberlake, topped the charts in the early 2000s?

source.notes: Went on indefinite hiatus in 2002; released 'No Strings Attached' (2000).

questions.q: In what year was the first iPod released?

source.notes: October 23, 2001; offered 5 GB of storage.

questions.q: The first Harry Potter film, 'The Philosopher's/Sorcerer's Stone', was released in which year?

source.notes: Directed by Chris Columbus; introduced Daniel Radcliffe as Harry.

questions.q: In what year was Facebook launched from Mark Zuckerberg's Harvard dorm room?

source.notes: Originally called 'TheFacebook'; launched February 4, 2004.

questions.q: YouTube was founded in what year?

source.notes: Co-founded February 2005 by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim.

questions.q: Twitter was launched in which year?

source.notes: Co-founded by Jack Dorsey, Noah Glass, Biz Stone, and Evan Williams.

questions.q: 'Seven Nation Army', the stadium-chant anthem of the 2000s, was recorded by which duo?

source.notes: From the 2003 album 'Elephant'; Jack and Meg White.

questions.q: Which duo had a double-header smash in 2003 with 'Hey Ya!' and 'The Way You Move'?

source.notes: From their double album 'Speakerboxxx/The Love Below'.

questions.q: Barack Obama was first elected as U.S. President in which year?

source.notes: Defeated John McCain on November 4, 2008.

questions.q: Evanescence's 2003 breakthrough hit, featuring a rapping Paul McCoy, was titled what?
choices: Wake Me Up Inside (Bring Me to Life)

source.notes: Featured on the Daredevil soundtrack in 2003.

questions.q: Jay-Z teamed up with which artist on the 2009 hit 'Empire State of Mind'?

source.notes: Named New York City's anthem by then-Mayor Bloomberg.

questions.q: Amy Winehouse's 2006 Grammy-winning album had which haunting title?

source.notes: Won five Grammys in 2008 including Record of the Year ('Rehab').

questions.q: Jack Black starred as wannabe rock teacher Dewey Finn in which 2003 comedy?

source.notes: Directed by Richard Linklater.

questions.q: The final book in J.K. Rowling's Harry Potter series, published in 2007, is titled what?

source.notes: Published July 21, 2007; sold 8.3 million US copies on the first day.

questions.q: Which devastating hurricane struck New Orleans and the U.S. Gulf Coast in August 2005?

source.notes: Caused over 1,800 deaths and roughly $125 billion in damage.

questions.q: The US-led invasion of Iraq, beginning the Iraq War, was launched in which year?

source.notes: Invasion began March 20, 2003.

questions.q: James Cameron's 'Avatar' was released in December 2009 and set box office records in which film format?

source.notes: Grossed over $2.9 billion worldwide; popularized mainstream 3D.

questions.q: Danger Mouse's 'The Grey Album' (2004) famously mashed up Jay-Z's 'Black Album' with songs from which source?

source.notes: Unofficial mashup; EMI (copyright-holder for Beatles recordings) tried to block its distribution.
